source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The space between two long thin metal cylinders is filled with a material with dielectric constant $\varepsilon$. The cylinders have radii $a$ and $b$, as shown in Fig. 1.19. What is the charge per unit length on the cylinders when the potential between them is $V$ with the outer cylinder at the higher potential?

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: The space between two long thin metal cylinders is filled with a material with dielectric constant $\varepsilon$. The cylinders have radii $a$ and $b$, as shown in Fig. 1.19. What is the charge per unit length on the cylinders when the potential between them is $V$ with the outer cylinder at the higher potential?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: images字段和fallback.image_paths中提供的是PIL对象字符串表示，不是实际文件路径或URL
题目提到Fig. 1.19，但记录中vision_relevance为optional，未判断为明显必须依赖图像
metadata.reasoning_chain: This is a cylindrical coaxial capacitor with a capacitance per unit length of $C=\frac{2 \pi \varepsilon}{\ln \left(\frac{a}{b}\right)}$. As the outer cylinder is at the higher potential, we have from $Q=C V$ the charges per unit length on the inner and outer cylinders: $\lambda_{\mathrm{i}}=-\frac{2 \pi \varepsilon V}{\ln \left(\frac{a}{b}\right)}$, $\lambda_{\mathrm{o}}=\frac{2 \pi \varepsilon V}{\ln \left(\frac{a}{b}\right)}$.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: This is a cylindrical coaxial capacitor with a capacitance per unit length of $C=\frac{2 \pi \varepsilon}{\ln \left(\frac{a}{b}\right)}$. As the outer cylinder is at the higher potential, we have from $Q=C V$ the charges per unit length on the inner and outer cylinders: $\lambda_{\mathrm{i}}=-\frac{2 \pi \varepsilon V}{\ln \left(\frac{a}{b}\right)}$, $\lambda_{\mathrm{o}}=\frac{2 \pi \varepsilon V}{\ln \left(\frac{a}{b}\right)}$.
